ontologistAudit.securityDomain: none — no RBAC/access-control concerns in content delivery pipeline
ontologistAudit.keyInsight: CSS3DRenderer is architecturally excluded (postprocessing incompatible). troika-three-text is the only WebGL-native text approach compatible with n8ao pipeline (via OverrideMaterialManager workaround). Design quality (CTML compliance) controls cognitive load, not rendering approach immersion level. CRA Concrete phase has CTML SpatialContiguity violation in current implementation.

objects.name: CSS3DObject
objects.description: Three.js CSS3DRenderer element — DOM in 3D space via perspective transforms. Separate from WebGL pipeline, CANNOT receive postprocessing. Z-ordering with WebGL objects is unreliable. EXCLUDED from viable set for mathcrew.
objects.semanticHeuristic: SH-02: CSS3DRenderer capability properties (DOM-based, no WebGL pipeline, no postprocessing) describe what it IS. Classification: DATA.
objects.exclusionRationale: clm-ext-12 + clm-ext-38: CSS3DRenderer CANNOT receive WebGL postprocessing (architectural impossibility confirmed by Three.js core developer Mugen87). mathcrew uses n8ao + postprocessing 6.36.7 — incompatible.

designPrinciples.DDD: PASS — CSS3DObject matches Three.js API vocabulary
designPrinciples.DRY: PASS — documented once as excluded option
designPrinciples.OCP: N/A — excluded from viable set
designPrinciples.PECS: N/A — excluded from viable set

forwardPropPath.chain: CSS3DObject (DATA) -> EXCLUDED — cannot participate in n8ao postprocessing pipeline

backwardPropPath.chain: N/A — excluded

objects.name: ContentDeliveryResolver
objects.description: NEW: Selects rendering approach per teaching context. Maps CRA phase → delivery mode: Concrete=3D spatial annotations, Representational=hybrid (troika labels + speech bubble), Abstract=speech bubble text. Implements OCP via Interface.
objects.semanticHeuristic: SH-03: Returns approach selection (Edits[]) without committing side effects — computes which renderer to use. SH-01: HOW TO REASON about approach selection given vtDimension, craPhase, R signal. Classification: LOGIC.

craMapping.Concrete: SpatialAnnotation on manipulable 3D object (NOT text bubble — CTML SpatialContiguity)

objects.name: CognitiveLoadGuard
objects.description: NEW: Ensures content delivery respects CLT. Rules: (1) Elementary requires high guidance, (2) Redundancy principle — no duplicate text+narration, (3) Coherence — no decorative vocabulary, (4) Segmenting — learner-controlled beats.
objects.semanticHeuristic: SH-01: HOW TO REASON about whether content delivery violates cognitive load constraints. SH-03: Returns validation result (no side effects). Classification: LOGIC.
objects.keyInsight: clm-ext-27: Design quality controls cognitive load, not rendering approach. Enriching delivery does NOT inherently increase extraneous load IF CTML principles are followed.

designPrinciples.DDD: PASS — CLT/CTML vocabulary preserved (redundancy, coherence, segmenting)
designPrinciples.DRY: PASS — single guard evaluates all CTML constraints
designPrinciples.OCP: PASS — new principles (Modality) added without breaking existing 4 rules
designPrinciples.PECS: PASS — ContentDeliveryResolver (producer) submits design for validation; guard (consumer super) checks

forwardPropPath.chain: CognitiveLoadGuard (LOGIC) -> validation result -> ContentDeliveryResolver blocks violation before render
gaps: Guard not implemented — CTML violations currently unchecked

backwardPropPath.chain: CTMLViolation event -> EvaluationRecord -> content design refinement
gaps: Automated CTML violation detection not in eval suite

objects.name: MayerPrincipleChecker
objects.description: NEW: Validates content delivery design against 5 applicable Mayer CTML principles. SpatialContiguity (labels near objects), TemporalContiguity (animation+text sync), Signalling (3D glow/highlight), Coherence (no extraneous info), Segmenting (beat-paced learner control).
objects.semanticHeuristic: SH-03: Returns compliance result per principle (no side effects). SH-01: HOW TO REASON about design quality against CTML standards. Classification: LOGIC.
currentViolations: SpatialContiguity: CRA Concrete phase uses text bubble instead of spatial annotation anchored on manipulated object
Signalling: No 3D glow/highlight on referenced math objects — only text references them

designPrinciples.DDD: PASS — Mayer published principle names preserved exactly
designPrinciples.DRY: PASS — single checker referenced by resolver, content design rules, and eval criteria
designPrinciples.OCP: PASS — new principles added without breaking existing 5
designPrinciples.PECS: N/A — design constraint checker, no generic boundary

forwardPropPath.chain: MayerPrincipleChecker (LOGIC) -> 5 compliance booleans -> CognitiveLoadGuard -> ContentDeliveryResolver.validate()
gaps: Signalling check requires 3D object reference tracking not yet implemented

backwardPropPath.chain: PrincipleViolation events -> EvaluationRecord -> design refinement proposals

objects.name: ScaffoldEscalationLogic

objects.semanticHeuristic: SH-03: Aggregates FSRS mastery signals over collection to derive scaffold tier recommendation (returns Edits[], no mutation). Classification: LOGIC (Reducer — aggregation without side effects).

objects.name: TroikaTextSync
objects.description: NEW: Async SDF text update via .sync(callback). Web worker generates SDF atlas off main thread. Must preloadFont() for Korean/math characters to prevent first-render latency. Beat-advance guard must await sync before displaying.
objects.semanticHeuristic: SH-03: Commits edits — syncing troika text CHANGES REALITY by updating Text mesh geometry in Three.js scene. Classification: ACTION.
objects.criticalConstraint: Beat-advance guard in game-loop.ts must check sync state — Text mesh must not be shown until sync(callback) fires. preloadFont() at scene init prevents first-beat latency.

designPrinciples.OCP: PASS — callback-based design; sync mechanism replaceable without breaking callers

objects.name: ResearchGapRegistry

objects.semanticHeuristic: SH-05: Captures WHAT WAS LEARNED about the limits of current evidence. Classification: LEARN.

ddd.analysis: Maps CRA/VT domain concepts to rendering decisions — domain vocabulary (Concrete, Separation) not tech vocabulary (troika vs CSS).
Domain terms: nudge, visual hint, worked example, direct instruction — pedagogical escalation language preserved.
Mayer's published principle names (SpatialContiguity, Segmenting, Coherence, Signalling, TemporalContiguity) preserved exactly.
Contrast, Separation, Generalization, Fusion, Integration — exact Variation Theory vocabulary.

dry.analysis: Single dispatch eliminates per-beat approach selection scattered in game-loop.
Single checker referenced by resolver, content design rules, and eval criteria — not duplicated per principle.
Consolidates MiniHudTelemetry + new content delivery telemetry into single record — no parallel truth sources.
Single compatibility matrix — resolver reads it; approach implementations don't duplicate compatibility checks.

ocp.analysis: Open for new approach variants (WebGPU text); closed for modification — existing speech bubble path unchanged.
Tier 0 (proactive encouragement) addable without modifying existing 4-tier logic.
New signal types (haptic, sound) added via animationTypes enum; existing glow/particle implementations unchanged.
New gaps added without changing existing gap structure.

pecs.analysis: ts-fsrs (producer) extends with R/S values; ScaffoldEscalationLogic (consumer super) reads R — new FSRS-7 fields don't break consumer.
Planet content (producer) extends TeachingBeat with new fields (audioNarrationUrl, spatialAnnotations); ContentDeliveryResolver (consumer super) reads via interface — additive, not breaking.
game-loop (producer) extends SpeechBubbleContent with referencedObjectId; speech-bubble.ts/diegetic-panel.ts (consumer super) ignore unknown fields.
